steps.title: Define Variables and Equation
steps.content: Let the smaller integer be $ x $. The larger integer is $ 2x + 11 $. Their product is $ x(2x + 11) = 546 $.

steps.title: Form Quadratic Equation
steps.content: Expand and rearrange the equation: $$2x^2 + 11x - 546 = 0$$. Apply the quadratic formula $ x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a} $ where $ a = 2 $, $ b = 11 $, $ c = -546 $.

steps.title: Calculate Discriminant and Roots
steps.content: Compute the discriminant: $ 121 + 4368 = 4489 $. The square root of 4489 is 67. Roots: $$x = \frac{-11 + 67}{4} = 14\quad\text{and}\quad x = \frac{-11 - 67}{4} = -19.5$$. Only $ x = 14 $ is a valid positive integer.

steps.title: Identify Smaller Integer
steps.content: The smaller integer is $ x = 14 $. Verify the product: $ 14 \times (2 \times 14 + 11) = 14 \times 39 = 546 $. That matches choice B — B is correct.
